2604.22942.title: VS-DDPM: Efficient Low-Cost Diffusion Model for Medical Modality Translation
2604.22942.paper_summary: Diffusion models produce high-quality synthetic data but suffer from slow inference. We propose 3D Variable-Step Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (VS-DDPM) a framework engineered to maintain generative quality while accelerating inference by several factors. We tested our approach on four tasks (missing MRI, tumor removal, MRI-to-sCT, and CBCT-to-sCT) within the BraTS2025 and SynthRAD2025 challenges. Designed for high efficiency under hardware and time constrains imposed by both challenges. VS-DDPM achieved state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in missing MRI synthesis, yielding Dice scores of 0.80, 0.83, and 0.88 for the enhancing tumor, tumor core, and whole tumor regions, respectively, alongside a structural similarity index (SSIM) of 0.95. For MRI tumor removal, the model attained a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.053, a peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of 26.77, and an SSIM of 0.918. While the framework demonstrated competitive performance in MRI-to-sCT and CBCT-to-sCT tasks, it did not reach SOTA benchmarks, potentially due to sensitivities in data pre and post-processing pipelines or specific loss function configurations. These results demonstrate that VS-DDPM provides a robust and tunable solution for high-fidelity 3D medical image synthesis. The code is available in https://github.com/andre-fs-ferreira/SynthRAD_by_Faking_it.
2604.22942.paper_summary_zh: 擴散模型生成高品質的合成數據，但推理速度較慢。我們提出了3D可變步長去噪擴散概率模型（VS-DDPM），這是一個旨在保持生成質量的同時加速推理的框架。我們在BraTS2025和SynthRAD2025挑戰中的四個任務（缺失MRI、腫瘤去除、MRI到sCT、CBCT到sCT）上測試了我們的方法。該模型設計為在兩個挑戰所施加的硬體和時間限制下具有高效率。VS-DDPM在缺失MRI合成中達到了最先進的（SOTA）性能，對於增強腫瘤、腫瘤核心和整個腫瘤區域，分別產生了0.80、0.83和0.88的Dice分數，以及0.95的結構相似性指數（SSIM）。在MRI腫瘤去除方面，該模型達到了0.053的均方根誤差（RMSE）、26.77的峰值信噪比（PSNR）和0.918的SSIM。雖然該框架在MRI到sCT和CBCT到sCT任務中顯示出競爭性能，但未達到SOTA基準，這可能是由於數據預處理和後處理管道的敏感性或特定損失函數配置所致。這些結果表明，VS-DDPM為高保真3D醫學影像合成提供了一個穩健且可調的解決方案。代碼可在 https://github.com/andre-fs-ferreira/SynthRAD_by_Faking_it 獲得。
2604.22942.author: Nikoo Moradi et.al.
2604.22942.authors: Nikoo Moradi, Gijs Luijten, Behrus Hinrichs-Puladi, Jens Kleesiek, Victor Alves, Jan Egger, André Ferreira
